page: Google Cloud Platform m1-megamem-96 is a memory-optimized server instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon processors, consisting of 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The instance is equipped with 1434.0 GB of RAM, providing approximately 14.93 GB of memory per core. It does not include any local storage or GPUs, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data shows strong multi-core and memory performance alongside weaker single-core capabilities. The server achieves strong results in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and passmark CPU extended instructions. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 3918.5 GB/sec and large block uncached write bandwidth at 89.97 GB/sec. Conversely, single-core performance is weak, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core score of 1273.18 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1026.0. Redis SET operations perform exceptionally well at 60.51 million operations per second, while LLM text generation speeds remain weak.

From a resource density perspective, the m1-megamem-96 offers high memory capacity relative to its compute resources, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require high single-threaded performance or local NVMe storage. The primary tradeoff is the lower single-core processing speed and the lack of bundled local storage. This hardware profile is best suited for large-scale in-memory databases, memory-intensive data processing, Redis caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring massive RAM footprints.
description: Google Cloud Platform m1-megamem-96 is a memory-optimized server featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs and 1434.0 GB of RAM on an x86_64 Intel Xeon architecture. Designed for memory-intensive workloads, it provides 48 physical cores with hyper-threading and a substantial 38 MB L3 cache. Benchmark results highlight strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth, with cached read speeds reaching 3918.5 GB/sec and Redis SET operations executing at 60.51 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is weak, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 1026.0. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, presenting a clear tradeoff between massive memory capacity and single-threaded processing speed. It is best suited for large in-memory databases, enterprise applications, and extensive caching layers.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m1-megamem-96 is a memory-optimized server with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 1.4 TB of RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for large-scale databases.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform m1-megamem-96 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs, 1.4 TB of RAM, and no local storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized server featuring ninety-six dedicated vCPUs and over one point four terabytes of RAM for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 1434.0 GB of RAM
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark results
Weak single-core performance and no bundled local storage
Delivers 60.51 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Suited for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise caching
